title: Key Google Rank Factors
content_md:
# Key Google Rank Factors

transcript.text: Google is math. Okay, and when I say that what I mean, let me move this
That Google does not hire people to read everything on the internet and decide which is better or which is worse
But Google cares very much about making sure that its searchers its users are happy
Something like 80% of alphabet Google's parent company something like 80% of their revenue comes from ads
So if people aren't seeing the ads alphabet doesn't make any money
so Google's free service search is paid for by
getting people to use it and
See and click on ads. So Google cares very much about keeping its users happy keeping its search
Quality results high. Okay, so the way that we keep a searcher happy is by serving them
URLs websites that answer their search query, okay, and what that means
We sometimes you call this open book SEO if we have a keyword that we want to rank for
We do not need to guess how to rank for that keyword
Okay, Google will tell us if we type that keyword into Google
Then Google will show us the 10 best pages on the internet that answer that query what all of its searchers are looking for
Okay, so to keep this in mind
Some people imagine Google to be a computer program with a bunch of if then statements and we know that's not true
What Google actually is it's a machine learning algorithm with a goal maximizing program
so it basically is trying to maximize its searchers happiness and it's measuring searcher happiness by engagement and
It's doing that by playing with all of the variables that
Google can see on a website. Okay, so it's trying a bajillion. That's the technical term
It's trying a bajillion different things and seeing how they impact the goal
Things that improve user engagement improve the goal. They're kept things that make the goal worse are thrown away
So, you know, you've probably seen articles on the internet
Oh, the 200 ranking factors or 300 ranking factors or whatever
We know that Google doesn't look at 200 or 300 or 400 factors
Google looks at every single infinitesimal thing on your website to decide based on its data
That it has had over the years if that website will make its searchers happy
Okay, so the result of that and Google search engineers have said this over and over again
They cannot tell why one URL is ranked above a different URL the search engineers do not know with
Specificity why a given URL is ranked where it is Google is doing it Google's algorithm is doing it on its own
So the question then if there's a search query that you want to rank for how do you rank for it?
Well open book test, right? You do. It's already what ranking well, but just do it a little bit better
So we're gonna base what we do on what the websites that are already ranking are doing. Okay
So what that means is if we type this query into Google and we see a bunch of e-commerce
Websites a bunch of e-commerce category pages
We know that we're not going to be able to rank a blog post for that
And it's not that Google doesn't want a blog post for those searches
It's that Google's users want e-commerce category stores
Those are the websites that they're engaging with that they're interacting with so Google is going to show more and more of those
And the vice versa thing is true if you have an e-commerce store and you want to rank in category page
But you type that search into Google and all of the results are blogs. It's not going to work, right?
You're not going to be able to rank something that is different from what Google is already showing
Okay, so that's where we say your content needs to be written for Google
Okay, and also for searchers, but if it's not written for Google, then you'll never rank at all
Google's algorithm is incapable of reading anything and as I mentioned, right?
It doesn't have Google doesn't have employees reading content on the internet
It's judging the content with its algorithm and it's likely something similar to a content called LSI
Late in semantic indexing. It is not that this is an outdated concept
So Google is using something much fancier more modern than this
But LSI is a way to think about how Google can tell what
Content is about just using math
so it's trying to figure out what your content is about and
You doesn't want you to just keyword stuff all over the place
So it uses something similar to LSI keywords. So let me give you an example of what an LSI keyword is, right?
So they're not synonyms. So attorney and lawyer though. That's an example of synonyms LSI
They're not keywords are trying to rank for so for attorney and lawyer
Some LSI examples will be courtroom trial settlement judge decision binding contract big boat
Right, the things that you're going to talk about if you're talking about attorneys or lawyers
Let me give you another example. So say you want to rank for Star Wars, okay?
Now that's very competitive. That's a very competitive search term and if you jam the word keyword Star Wars in there 50 times
That's not going to be enough
But the LSI keywords, right? Luke Skywalker, Princess Leia, Darth Vader, the Death Star, Jedi, Jar Jar Binks,
Mark Hamill and so on and so forth and those are the LSI keywords where Google has analyzed the entire internet of content and
Figured out that content that talks about Luke Skywalker and Princess Leia and Darth Vader is actually about Star Wars
Okay, so that's similar to how Google's algorithm is probably working
So once we do that and we write for Google we write for Google's algorithm to make sure it knows what we want
What we're talking about then Google uses external links as a quality signal, right?
Because Google is math it can't tell if something is of a quality that I wrote
Something is of a quality that Chad GPT spit out or something was written by Ernest Hemingway, right?
So these external links is a sign of quality some other website an independent website that you don't own that you don't control
Decided to link to your URL to your content. That's a sign to Google that you must have good content
Okay, so those links combined with using LSI keywords is the fundamental of how we do SEO
Move that again
Let me just move it down here. Maybe this will work. Okay
So and what Google is doing is it's looking at the content that is already ranking well
The content that is making its searchers happy
It has user engagement data with and it's analyzing the word use usage word patterns word structure in that content
That's already ranking well and comparing that to your content and if you're way off you're never going to rank
Okay, so
It's deciding. Hey, how close is your word patterns your word usage to the content that Google knows is making its users happy
How many links do you have to determine that it's good quality content and is your content?
comprehensive, how is the topical relevancy and we can add that here and
Topical relevance
That's an important factor also
Okay, and once if you check all these boxes
Then you're going to get to the first page and once you're there
It's going to use user engagement data to verify all of these signals were accurate that you didn't fake all of these signals
Which you can do but it's very hard to fake user engagement data
So we call this the Google dance so Google put you on the first page almost as a trial
And it's going to see how your user engagement metrics are so it's going to put you to the first page
It's going to bring you back down. It's going to put you on the first page
It's going to bring you back down. It's going to put you on the first page
It's going to bring you back down
It's going to do that a little bit if you got those other signals correct to see how happy you're making its users
And if you're not making them happy, it's going to bring you down and then leave you down
If you're making the users happy, especially happier than the content that's already on the first page, you're going to stay there
Okay, so I've said it many times here. Let's see if I can set a record for it. Google cannot read
So the goal of your content is to convince Google that it will make its searchers happy
Then a quality external link will show Google that your content is good
then building topical relevance
to show Google you have expertise and
Finally at the end of the day when Google puts you on the first page your content actually needs to make its searchers happy

summary: This lesson explains that Google ranks websites based on user engagement and algorithmic factors rather than human review. It emphasizes writing content for both users and search engines, using LSI keywords and external links to improve ranking potential.
key_points: Google prioritizes user satisfaction through high engagement
Use LSI keywords to match content relevance
Content must align with top-ranking pages' patterns
User engagement data verifies SEO signals
